extra.regularisation_note: Synthetic-DID minimises an l2-regularised objective for the unit-weight vector, and the regularisation constant zeta is set differently in sp.synth(method='sdid') vs synthdid::synthdid_estimate. Both estimates land in the published Arkhangelsky-Athey-Hirshberg-Imbens-Wager (2021) -15-to-19 packs/capita neighbourhood for California Prop 99: sp returns -17.25 (SE 4.43, bootstrap), synthdid returns -15.95 (SE 2.63, placebo). Reviewers should treat this as the same regularisation-convention family as the classical-SCM gap reported in Module 07.
